title: Review and improve project plans with AI
description: Add an AI review flow that evaluates a proposed bead, epic, or subtree for missing steps, unrealistic scope, unclear acceptance criteria, dependency mistakes, and sequencing risk.
design: This is the PM-style review loop: critique the plan, suggest decomposition, identify blockers, and make the work more executable for coding agents.
acceptance_criteria: Users can request a plan review from a bead or hierarchy subtree; AI returns actionable findings and proposed changes; user can apply selected modifications; dependency and child issue suggestions are reviewable before mutation.

title: Suggest complete bead fields from partial drafts
description: Let the AI project manager fill in missing bead form fields from a partial draft, existing project context, and selected parent or board context.
design: Return structured field suggestions with rationale. Do not overwrite user-entered fields unless the user explicitly accepts a replacement.
acceptance_criteria: Users can ask for AI suggestions while creating or editing a bead; suggestions include description, acceptance criteria, labels, priority, issue type, parent, and dependencies where useful; user can accept individual fields or reject the proposal.

title: Add server-side LLM configuration and status
description: Add Mac-server-only configuration for local or remote LLM providers, including provider status, model selection, and safe failure behavior for clients.
design: Keep AI execution server-side. Clients should only know whether planning assistance is available and what actions can be requested.
acceptance_criteria: Mac server exposes LLM availability/status; configuration is not required on iOS or iPadOS; missing or failing LLM providers degrade gracefully; no API keys or local model paths are exposed to clients.
close_reason: Added Mac-server-only LLM provider configuration, sanitized availability status in health and /llm/status, client status display, and Go DTO/client support without exposing API keys.

title: Build AI project manager planning assistant
description: Add a server-side AI project manager capability that helps turn vague work into realistic beads, hierarchy, dependencies, risk notes, acceptance criteria, and progress reporting. The goal is not simple autocomplete; it should automate planning and reporting work that a project manager would normally perform.
design: AI PM should operate over canonical server state, repository context, and beads hierarchy. It should produce reviewable structured patches rather than silently mutating project state.
acceptance_criteria: Server owns AI configuration and execution; clients can request planning help; AI returns structured proposals for user review; proposals can create or update beads, parents, dependencies, and reporting summaries only after explicit user confirmation.

title: Add parent and child issue controls
description: Add buttonology and forms for creating child beads, selecting or changing a parent bead, and navigating between parent and child beads from cards and detail views.
design: Children remain independent cards in Kanban columns. Parent cards show compact child links/counts, and create-child defaults the parent field to the current bead.
acceptance_criteria: Bead editor supports selecting a parent; detail view has create-child and parent/child navigation controls; card previews show child counts and clickable child links where space allows; changes sync through the server model.
close_reason: Added parent selection, create-child flow, relationship navigation controls, and Kanban child link previews.

title: Add hierarchy-focused beads view
description: Add a cross-platform view that shows beads as an outline/tree instead of a Kanban board, using parent/child relationships while preserving quick navigation back to the board and selected bead detail.
design: Kanban remains the workflow view. Hierarchy view is a separate structural view for epics, milestones, and child work.
acceptance_criteria: Users can switch to a hierarchy view; parent beads expand to show children; rows expose type, status, priority, and blocked state; selecting a row navigates to the bead detail; iPhone, iPad, and macOS layouts are usable.
close_reason: Added a cross-platform hierarchy workspace with expandable parent/child rows, relationship-aware metadata chips, row selection into bead detail, iPhone navigation, and board/hierarchy switching.

title: Model beads hierarchy and dependency relationships
description: Preserve native beads structure in the app model and server payloads so parent/child hierarchy, issue type, and dependency relationships can drive UI and later planning automation.
design: Treat hierarchy and dependencies as separate concepts. Parent/child is structural ownership. Dependencies are workflow/blocking edges. Keep every bead movable independently on the Kanban board.
acceptance_criteria: Imported beads preserve beads ID, issue type, parent ID, dependency IDs/counts, and dependent IDs/counts; server/client JSON includes the relationship fields; existing boards migrate without data loss.
close_reason: Preserved native beads hierarchy and dependency metadata across import, model persistence, server JSON, Swift clients, and Go DTOs.

title: Add compact iOS board switcher
description: The iPhone compact client needs an obvious way to switch between boards from the main board screen. It currently shows whichever board is selected internally, making additional server boards hard to discover.
close_reason: Added compact iPhone board switcher menu with selected-board title.

title: Fix iOS client board discovery from Mac server
description: iOS clients should pick up boards created on the canonical Mac server during refresh/sync. Currently new server-side boards do not appear reliably on iOS.
close_reason: Fixed iOS/iPadOS paired-server refresh and protected server-only boards during client saves.

title: Add optimized iPad landscape workspace
description: Create a dedicated regular-width landscape iPad layout that is meaningfully different from portrait and gives the board more horizontal working space.
acceptance_criteria: Landscape iPad uses board + inspector without the persistent sidebar; portrait iPad keeps split-view behavior; builds pass.

title: Fix compact iPhone board navigation
description: Replace squashed split board with a compact one-column board flow.
acceptance_criteria: iPhone shows one column at a time, native toolbar/search, and pushes bead detail instead of showing squeezed split panes.
close_reason: Implemented compact iPhone one-column board flow with inline search and native detail navigation.

title: Tune iPad and macOS layouts
description: Improve regular-width iPad and macOS density, inspector, toolbar, and adaptive columns.
acceptance_criteria: iPad split view preserves board/detail clarity; macOS detail is leading-aligned and toolbar/sidebar/card density feel native.
close_reason: Tuned iPad/macOS board sizing and added macOS inspector layout.

title: Adaptive shells for iPhone, iPad, and macOS
description: Implement Apple HIG-driven adaptive layouts while preserving shared code reuse.
acceptance_criteria: iPhone uses compact one-column workflow; iPad uses optimized split view; macOS uses denser desktop inspector.
close_reason: Adaptive platform UI pass completed and builds verified.

title: Generate AI project status reports
description: Add AI-generated project reporting over boards, epics, and hierarchy subtrees, summarizing progress, risks, blockers, stale work, and likely next actions.
design: Reporting should use canonical server state plus hierarchy/dependency structure. It should be terse, operational, and focused on decisions and next actions.
acceptance_criteria: Users can generate a status report for a board or parent bead; report distinguishes completed, active, blocked, stale, and unplanned work; output can be copied or saved as notes; no project state is changed without confirmation.

title: Visualize selected parent relationships on the board
description: When a parent bead is selected, provide an optional board overlay that visually connects the parent card to visible child cards and indicates children hidden by scroll, filter, board, or column visibility.
design: Relationship lines are a temporary selection affordance, not always-on decoration. Avoid visual noise and preserve Kanban readability.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting a parent can show unobtrusive relationship lines to visible children; hidden children are summarized; overlay does not interfere with drag/drop, scrolling, or compact iPhone layouts; users can disable or ignore the overlay.

title: Add cross-platform Go client
description: Add a cross-platform Go command-line client in the same repository without wiring it into the Xcode project. It should build via Makefile and talk to the existing Beads-Orchestrator Mac server API.
close_reason: Added isolated Go CLI and Fyne UI clients with Makefile build support.

title: Design and wire app icon into Xcode project
description: Create a Beads-Orchestrator app icon and add it directly to the Xcode project asset catalogs for macOS, iOS, and iPadOS.
close_reason: Generated and wired shared AppIcon asset catalog for macOS, iOS, and iPadOS.
